affordances.name: Relative-position adaptation test
affordances.mechanism: When a team is working hard in a competitive environment, require the answer to distinguish maintenance effort from true advantage and decide where adaptation is non-optional versus where an arms race should be refused.

use_when: Standing still effectively means losing ground because rivals, substitutes, or environmental pressures are improving.
The team must distinguish maintenance effort from true advantage.
The decision needs to name where continuous adaptation is required and where competing is wasteful.
do_not_use_when: The situation is not relative-performance driven and effort can be judged on absolute outcomes.
Every challenge is being framed as an arms race.
The answer would chase feature parity or reactive speed instead of choosing where not to compete.
case_evidence_needed: The rival, substitute, benchmark, or environmental pressure that changes the relative standard.
The evidence that current effort merely maintains position.
The areas where adaptation is non-optional.
The areas where refusing the race would protect focus or advantage.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to identify whether the proposed effort maintains relative position, creates advantage, or merely reacts to competitor motion.
evidence_required: The competitive or environmental reference point.
The improvement rate of rivals, substitutes, or pressures.
The effort needed just to maintain position.
The strategic reason to adapt, ignore, or exit the race.
good_output_shape: Separates maintenance work from advantage-building work.

diagnostic_questions: Are we improving absolutely but falling behind relatively?
Which effort only keeps us in place, and which effort creates advantage?
Where are we chasing parity instead of choosing a better field of competition?
misuse_guards: Do not frame every challenge as an arms race.
Do not treat feature parity or reactive speed as strategy.
Do not ignore the cognitive exhaustion and analysis paralysis created by endless adaptation pressure.

source_evidence.source_quote: continuous adaptation and evolution are necessary just to maintain one’s current state
standing still effectively means losing ground
distinguish maintenance effort from true advantage

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 5 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports a relative-position adaptation test and an explicit guard against arms-race overuse.
dropped_material: Did not extract biological evolution examples or longevity arguments as separate affordances.
Did not infer competitor-strategy advice beyond source-supported relative-position discipline.
open_questions: Future packet review should test whether this card helps competitive strategy without encouraging reactive motion.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that treating every challenge as an arms race can cause reactive motion instead of focused strategy.

source_evidence.source_quote: every challenge gets framed as an arms race

absence_records.reason: The source rejects feature parity and reactive speed as substitutes for choosing where adaptation actually matters.

source_evidence.source_quote: feature parity, or reactive speed instead of choosing where not to compete
Its fundamental essence is the concept that **longevity does not protect entities from extinction**.
It functions primarily as an argument **against complacency**.

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 5 source reading found strong reviewed depth for Red Queen Effect. Extracted one relative-position test and rejected arms-race/feature-parity overuse.
dropped_material: Evolutionary examples and longevity warnings were kept as evidence, not separate operational cards.
No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet comparison should check whether this card improves competitive dynamics coverage without increasing reactive strategy bias.
